commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
West wind. Literally, Africum, which blows "from Africa "in this direction, with respect to Jerusalem. (Haydock)
The same wind may be styled the south wind. Hebrew Kadim, "strong, eastern "(Berthier)
These quails came from the banks of the southern ocean, or from the Red Sea, as the Israelites were still in Arabia, when they were furnished with them a second time, (Numbers xi. 31.) for a whole month, (Calmet) though there were about three million people. (Berthier)
God changed the wind, so as to bring them into the camp, Exodus xvi. (Worthington)
It was before blowing from the south-east. (Menochius)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
